title: Provide default for nullable injection
description: Nullable fields and parameters injected by frameworks like Spring should always have a default value. Without a default, the application may encounter unexpected nulls, leading to runtime errors and unstable behavior. Ensuring defaults improves reliability and code safety.
recommendation: Always assign a default value to injected fields or parameters that can be null.

noncompliant: @Autowired(required = false) private SomeService someService; // no default value
compliant: @Autowired(required = false) private SomeService someService = new DefaultService();

checks.message: Always assign a default value to injected fields or parameters that can be null.
